semantic_matched_intents.quote: Test your changes - Build and test on appropriate platforms

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Use `#` comments in shell scripts and YAML to explain non-obvious logic

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Never hardcode secrets: All secrets flow through Azure Key Vault or GitHub Secrets — never commit credentials, API keys, or passwords to the repository
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already never commits secrets by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Use existing utilities and shared modules when possible

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Refactor code unrelated to current requirements

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Output: Structured, clear, concise

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Understand before changing: Know how existing systems work first

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Test mentally first: Think through lifecycle and edge cases

semantic_matched_intents.quote: only commit what you're asked to commit, no adding extra changes unless asked
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already does not add features, refactors, or abstractions beyond what the task requires by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: when person asks the question, but not asking you to do the thing, you only answer the question, you don't do the thing you're not asked to do!
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already does not add features, refactors, or abstractions beyond what the task requires by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: STOP before any file edit. Ask yourself: "Did they explicitly say to make changes?" If no → DO NOT EDIT. Present findings and wait.
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already does not add features, refactors, or abstractions beyond what the task requires by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: "find", "check", "investigate", "research", "explain", "look into" = RESEARCH ONLY. Never edit code.
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already does not add features, refactors, or abstractions beyond what the task requires by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: The ONLY trigger for code changes is explicit words like: "do it", "fix it", "implement", "make the change", "go ahead"
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already does not add features, refactors, or abstractions beyond what the task requires by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Comments: Sparse - prefer self-documenting code, function-level docs only when needed
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already writes sparse comments, preferring self-documenting code, and only writes comments when the WHY is non-obvious by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: avoid lots of documentation unless helpful to LLMs.
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already avoids multi-paragraph docstrings and multi-line comment blocks, and only writes comments when the WHY is non-obvious by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Read the full current skill before editing
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already investigates unfamiliar state before deleting or overwriting by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Never: obvious comments
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already writes comments only when the WHY is non-obvious by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Default to no comments - clear code and good names are better
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already does not explain WHAT the code does with comments, preferring clear code and good names.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Avoid comments that restate the code - they become stale
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already does not explain WHAT the code does with comments.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Complex algorithms or non-obvious "gotchas"
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already only writes a comment when the WHY is non-obvious.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Avoid obvious branching comments - `if (x)` rarely needs `// when x is true`
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already does not explain WHAT the code does with comments.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Title: imperative mood, lowercase after colon, no period
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already uses imperative-mood summary for commit messages.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Keep bodies concise (1-2 lines typical, trivial changes need only a title)
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already writes 1–2 sentences for commit message summaries.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: The "why": motivation, design choices, preparation for future work
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already focuses commit messages on WHY not WHAT.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Avoid repo-wide formatting/unrelated refactors.
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already does not add features, refactors, or abstractions beyond what the task requires by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: ALWAYS prefer editing an existing file to creating a new one.
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already prefers editing existing files over creating new ones by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: NEVER proactively create documentation files (*.md) or README files. Only create documentation files if explicitly requested by the User.
